contract.name: v2/awards/count/federal_account/award_id.md
contract.description: Returns the number of federal accounts linked to an award when you provide USAspending's internal digit-only award identifier.

award_id.description: Digit-only path parameter that identifies the award in USAspending's internal award dataset.
award_id.constraints: Required. Provide decimal digits only. Unknown numeric ids return a JSON 404. Any non-digit character triggers an HTML 404. Omit the trailing slash and the server issues a 301 redirect.

federal_accounts.description: Non-negative count of distinct federal accounts linked to the award.

quirks: Trailing slash is mandatory; otherwise the service responds with HTTP 301 redirect to the slash-suffixed path.
Non-numeric award ids return HTML 404 pages instead of JSON error payloads.
Digit-only ids that are not present in the dataset return JSON 404 responses with a detail message.
POST requests receive HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed.
risks: Clients must resolve the internal numeric award id via another endpoint before calling this route; public award identifiers fail.
Error handling must accommodate both JSON and HTML 404 payloads depending on whether the award id matches the digit-only pattern.
Clients that do not automatically follow redirects will fail if they omit the trailing slash.
gaps: Exact numeric award id domain remains unclear; only sampled ids between roughly 200,000,000 and 350,239,000 were verified.

mismatches: Documentation claims the `award_id` accepts assistance identifiers such as 'ASST_NON_NNX17AJ96A_8000', but the live API returns 404 for that value.
Docs describe `award_id` as an assistance or contract identifier string, yet probes show only digit-only internal award ids succeed.
gaps: Exact numeric award id domain remains unclear; only sampled ids between roughly 200,000,000 and 350,239,000 were verified.
risks: Clients must resolve the internal numeric award id via another endpoint before calling this route; public award identifiers fail.
Error handling must accommodate both JSON and HTML 404 payloads depending on whether the award id matches the digit-only pattern.
Clients that do not automatically follow redirects will fail if they omit the trailing slash.
